title: The History of the Olympic Games
summary: A narrator traces the origins of the Olympic Games in ancient Greece, their revival in the modern era, and how the games have evolved into a global celebration of sport and human achievement.
body: The Olympic Games are one of the oldest and most enduring sporting traditions in human history. Their origins date back to ancient Greece, where the first recorded games were held at Olympia in 776 BCE. These early games were not just athletic competitions — they were religious festivals dedicated to Zeus, the king of the Greek gods. Athletes from across the Greek world travelled to Olympia every four years to compete, and a sacred truce was observed during the festival, meaning that wars were temporarily paused so that competitors and spectators could travel safely. The original Olympic events included running, jumping, wrestling, discus throwing, and chariot racing. Athletes competed in the nude as a sign of physical perfection and in honour of the gods. Women were not permitted to compete or even watch most events. The ancient games continued for over a thousand years before they were abolished by the Roman Emperor Theodosius I in 393 CE, who viewed them as a pagan practice. For almost fifteen hundred years, the Olympic Games did not exist. Their revival came in 1896, largely thanks to the efforts of a French nobleman named Pierre de Coubertin, who believed that sport could promote international peace and understanding. The first modern Olympics were held in Athens, Greece, with fourteen nations participating and around two hundred and forty athletes, all of whom were male. Since then, the games have grown enormously. Today, more than two hundred nations send athletes to compete, and the games are watched by billions of people around the world via television and the internet. Women began competing at the Olympic Games in 1900 at the Paris Olympics, first in events such as tennis and golf. The Summer and Winter Olympics now alternate every two years, and the Paralympic Games, for athletes with disabilities, have become a major event in their own right. The modern Olympics have not been without controversy. They have been affected by political boycotts, doping scandals, and debates about commercialisation. Yet the ideals that inspired the revival — the pursuit of excellence, the celebration of human potential, and the hope for peaceful competition between nations — continue to draw athletes and audiences from every corner of the globe.

annotations.meaningVi: bền bỉ nhất, lâu dài nhất
annotations.exampleEn: Friendship is one of the most enduring bonds between human beings.

annotations.meaningVi: lệnh ngừng chiến thiêng liêng, hiệp ước ngừng bắn tạm thời mang tính tôn giáo
annotations.exampleEn: A sacred truce was declared so that pilgrims could travel to the temple safely.

annotations.meaningVi: tạm thời dừng lại, ngừng trong một khoảng thời gian nhất định
annotations.exampleEn: Construction was temporarily paused due to the unexpected discovery of ancient ruins.

annotations.meaningVi: thi đấu trong trạng thái không có quần áo
annotations.exampleEn: Ancient Greek athletes competed in the nude as an expression of ideal physical form.

annotations.meaningVi: nghi lễ ngoại đạo, phong tục của người không theo đạo Thiên Chúa
annotations.exampleEn: The emperor banned many pagan practices after converting to Christianity.

annotations.meaningVi: sự hồi sinh, sự phục hưng
annotations.exampleEn: The revival of interest in classical music has been remarkable in recent years.

annotations.meaningVi: thúc đẩy hòa bình quốc tế
annotations.exampleEn: Cultural exchange programmes aim to promote international peace and understanding.
Over fifty countries are participating in this year's trade fair.

annotations.meaningVi: tẩy chay chính trị, từ chối tham gia vì lý do chính trị
annotations.exampleEn: Several countries announced political boycotts of the games in protest.

annotations.meaningVi: các bê bối doping, sự cố liên quan đến sử dụng chất cấm trong thể thao
annotations.exampleEn: Doping scandals have damaged the reputation of several high-profile athletes.

annotations.meaningVi: thương mại hóa, quá trình biến thứ gì đó thành mục tiêu kiếm tiền
annotations.exampleEn: The commercialisation of professional sport has raised questions about its original values.

annotations.meaningVi: sự theo đuổi sự xuất sắc, nỗ lực đạt đến đỉnh cao
annotations.exampleEn: The pursuit of excellence drives athletes to train harder every day.

annotations.meaningVi: tiềm năng con người, khả năng của con người
annotations.exampleEn: Education is the best way to unlock human potential.

annotations.meaningVi: luân phiên cứ hai năm một lần
annotations.exampleEn: The competitions alternate every two years between different host cities.

annotations.meaningVi: theo đúng nghĩa của nó, đủ tiêu chuẩn tự thân
annotations.exampleEn: She is a talented artist in her own right, not just because of her famous parents.

grammarNotes.title: Past Simple vs Past Perfect — Sequencing historical events
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong bài viết lịch sử, thì quá khứ đơn và quá khứ hoàn thành được dùng để sắp xếp trình tự các sự kiện. Ví dụ: "the first recorded games were held at Olympia in 776 BCE" (quá khứ đơn — sự kiện lịch sử đã hoàn tất), "they were abolished by the Roman Emperor Theodosius I in 393 CE" (quá khứ đơn). Thì quá khứ hoàn thành xuất hiện khi cần nhấn mạnh một hành động xảy ra trước một hành động quá khứ khác: "Women began competing at the Olympic Games in 1900... first in events such as tennis and golf" — có thể so sánh với "who believed that sport could promote international peace" (quá khứ trong câu tường thuật).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Historical writing relies on the simple past for completed events ('were held,' 'were abolished') and occasionally the past perfect to show prior events ('had occurred before...'). In this passage, reported speech in the past uses backshifted tenses: 'who believed that sport could promote peace' — the original belief ('sport can promote peace') shifts to 'could' in past reported speech. This is important for understanding indirect speech in historical writing.

grammarNotes.title: Passive Voice — History and processes without a named agent
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thể bị động xuất hiện nhiều trong bài để mô tả sự kiện lịch sử khi không cần hoặc không biết đến tác nhân. Ví dụ: "the first recorded games were held at Olympia", "a sacred truce was observed", "they were abolished by the Roman Emperor", "Women were not permitted to compete". Cấu trúc: was/were + past participle. Khi người thực hiện quan trọng, dùng 'by' để chỉ ra ("abolished by Theodosius I"); khi không quan trọng, bỏ qua.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The passive voice dominates historical writing when the focus is on what happened rather than who caused it. 'Were held,' 'was observed,' 'were abolished,' and 'were not permitted' all describe events where either the agent is unknown, unimportant, or already established. When the agent matters, 'by' introduces it ('abolished by Theodosius I'). Recognising this pattern helps comprehension of formal historical texts.

translationVi:
Thế vận hội Olympic là một trong những truyền thống thể thao lâu đời và bền bỉ nhất trong lịch sử nhân loại. Nguồn gốc của nó có từ Hy Lạp cổ đại, nơi các trò chơi được ghi chép đầu tiên được tổ chức tại Olympia vào năm 776 trước Công nguyên. Những trò chơi đầu tiên này không chỉ là các cuộc thi thể thao — chúng là các lễ hội tôn giáo dành riêng cho Zeus, vua của các vị thần Hy Lạp. Các vận động viên từ khắp thế giới Hy Lạp đã đến Olympia bốn năm một lần để thi đấu, và một lệnh ngừng chiến thiêng liêng được tuân thủ trong lễ hội, nghĩa là các cuộc chiến tranh tạm thời dừng lại để các đối thủ và khán giả có thể đi lại an toàn.

Các môn thi đấu Olympic ban đầu bao gồm chạy, nhảy, đấu vật, ném đĩa và đua xe ngựa. Các vận động viên thi đấu khỏa thân như một dấu hiệu của sự hoàn hảo thể chất và để tôn vinh các vị thần. Phụ nữ không được phép thi đấu hay thậm chí xem hầu hết các môn thi. Các trò chơi cổ đại tiếp tục kéo dài hơn một nghìn năm trước khi bị bãi bỏ bởi Hoàng đế La Mã Theodosius I vào năm 393 sau Công nguyên, người coi chúng là tập tục ngoại đạo.

Gần mười lăm trăm năm, Thế vận hội không tồn tại. Sự hồi sinh của chúng đến vào năm 1896, phần lớn nhờ nỗ lực của một quý tộc người Pháp tên là Pierre de Coubertin, người tin rằng thể thao có thể thúc đẩy hòa bình và sự hiểu biết quốc tế. Thế vận hội hiện đại đầu tiên được tổ chức tại Athens, Hy Lạp, với mười bốn quốc gia tham dự và khoảng hai trăm bốn mươi vận động viên, tất cả đều là nam giới.

Kể từ đó, các trò chơi đã phát triển đáng kể. Ngày nay, hơn hai trăm quốc gia cử vận động viên thi đấu, và các trò chơi được theo dõi bởi hàng tỷ người trên thế giới qua truyền hình và internet. Phụ nữ bắt đầu thi đấu tại Thế vận hội năm 1900 tại Paris, đầu tiên trong các môn như quần vợt và golf. Thế vận hội mùa hè và mùa đông giờ luân phiên cứ hai năm một lần, và Thế vận hội Paralympic, dành cho vận động viên khuyết tật, đã trở thành sự kiện lớn theo đúng nghĩa của nó.

Thế vận hội hiện đại không phải không có tranh cãi. Chúng đã bị ảnh hưởng bởi các cuộc tẩy chay chính trị, bê bối doping và các cuộc tranh luận về thương mại hóa. Tuy nhiên, những lý tưởng đã thúc đẩy sự hồi sinh — việc theo đuổi sự xuất sắc, tôn vinh tiềm năng con người và hy vọng về sự cạnh tranh hòa bình giữa các quốc gia — tiếp tục thu hút vận động viên và khán giả từ mọi nơi trên thế giới.

questions.prompt: When were the first recorded Olympic Games held?
questions.explanation: The passage states 'the first recorded games were held at Olympia in 776 BCE.'
questions.hint: The date is mentioned in the opening paragraph.

questions.prompt: What were the ancient Olympic Games dedicated to?
options: Zeus, king of the Greek gods
questions.explanation: The passage says the games were 'religious festivals dedicated to Zeus, the king of the Greek gods.'
questions.hint: Think about which god was associated with Olympia.

questions.prompt: What was the purpose of the sacred truce during the ancient Olympics?
options: To allow athletes to rest before competing
To allow competitors and spectators to travel safely
To prevent cheating and corruption during the games
To honour the gods with a period of silence and prayer
questions.explanation: The passage says 'wars were temporarily paused so that competitors and spectators could travel safely.'
questions.hint: The truce had a practical purpose related to movement and safety.

questions.prompt: Why did athletes in the ancient games compete without clothing?
options: Because clothes were too expensive for most athletes
To make judging easier and more accurate
As a sign of physical perfection and in honour of the gods
Because the hot climate made wearing clothes impractical
questions.explanation: The passage says athletes 'competed in the nude as a sign of physical perfection and in honour of the gods.'
questions.hint: The reason was both symbolic and religious.

questions.prompt: Why were the ancient Olympic Games abolished in 393 CE?
options: Because the Roman Empire could no longer afford them
Because they had become too violent
Because the Emperor Theodosius viewed them as a pagan practice
Because too many athletes were cheating
questions.explanation: The passage says Emperor Theodosius I abolished the games 'who viewed them as a pagan practice.'
questions.hint: Think about the religious context of the Roman Empire in the fourth century.

questions.prompt: Who is credited with reviving the modern Olympic Games?
options: A French nobleman named Pierre de Coubertin
A British aristocrat named Thomas Arnold
A German politician named Wilhelm von Humboldt
questions.explanation: The passage credits 'a French nobleman named Pierre de Coubertin' with the revival of the games.
questions.hint: Think about the nationality and title of the person involved.

questions.prompt: Where were the first modern Olympic Games held?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'The first modern Olympics were held in Athens, Greece.'
questions.hint: The location was chosen to honour the games' ancient origins.

questions.prompt: When did women first begin competing in the Olympic Games?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'Women began competing at the Olympic Games in 1900 at the Paris Olympics.'
questions.hint: Women were not included in the very first modern Olympics.

questions.prompt: How do the Summer and Winter Olympics currently relate to each other?
options: They are held in the same year in different cities
The Winter Olympics replaced the Summer Olympics
The Summer Olympics are held every four years and the Winter Olympics every two years
questions.explanation: The passage says 'The Summer and Winter Olympics now alternate every two years.'
questions.hint: Think about how frequently each set of games is held.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a controversy affecting the modern Olympics?
options: Arguments about the host city selection process
questions.explanation: The passage mentions boycotts, doping, and commercialisation as controversies, but does not mention the host city selection process.
questions.hint: Look at what is explicitly listed as controversies in the passage.

questions.prompt: What three ideals does the narrator say still inspire the modern Olympic Games?
options: National pride, athletic perfection, and global friendship
The pursuit of excellence, the celebration of human potential, and hope for peaceful competition
Physical health, international cooperation, and sporting records
Religious devotion, cultural exchange, and economic development
questions.explanation: The narrator concludes with 'the pursuit of excellence, the celebration of human potential, and the hope for peaceful competition between nations.'
questions.hint: These three ideals are listed in the final sentence of the passage.

cloze.template: The Olympic Games are one of the oldest and most {{b1}} sporting traditions in human history. Their origins date back to ancient Greece, where the first recorded games were held at Olympia in 776 BCE. These early games were religious festivals {{b2}} to Zeus, the king of the Greek gods. A sacred {{b3}} was observed during the festival, meaning that wars were temporarily paused so that competitors and spectators could travel safely. The ancient games were {{b4}} by the Roman Emperor Theodosius I in 393 CE, who viewed them as a pagan practice. Their {{b5}} came in 1896, largely thanks to a French nobleman named Pierre de Coubertin, who believed that sport could {{b6}} international peace and understanding. The first modern Olympics were held in Athens with fourteen nations {{b7}}. Women began competing at the Olympic Games in 1900 at the Paris Olympics. The Summer and Winter Olympics now {{b8}} every two years, and the Paralympic Games have become a major event in their own {{b9}}. The modern Olympics have been affected by political {{b10}}, doping scandals, and debates about {{b11}}. Yet the ideals of excellence, human potential, and peaceful competition continue to draw athletes from every corner of the globe.

blanks.explanation: 'Most enduring' means lasting for the longest time — the opening describes the Olympics as one of the oldest and most long-lasting traditions.
'Dedicated to' means created or intended as an honour for someone or something — here the games honoured Zeus.
'Sacred truce' is the specific term for the temporary peace declared during the ancient Olympics to ensure safe passage for participants.
'Abolished' means formally and permanently ended — a stronger and more official word than 'cancelled' or 'stopped.'
'Revival' means bringing something back to life after a period of absence — the exact term used in the passage for the 1896 games.
'Promote international peace' means to actively support and encourage peace between nations — a standard collocation in political and humanitarian language.
'Participating' means taking part in an organised event — the natural verb to use when describing nations joining the Olympics.
'Alternate' means to take turns in a regular pattern — here the Summer and Winter Olympics take turns every two years.
'In their own right' is a fixed phrase meaning independently worthy or significant — the Paralympics are not just an add-on but a major event by themselves.
'Political boycotts' means refusing to participate for political reasons — a specific term for this type of protest at international sporting events.
'Commercialisation' refers to the process of making something primarily focused on making money — a recurring debate in discussions about professional sport.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator mentions that the modern Olympics have faced boycotts, doping scandals, and commercialisation, yet argues they still embody important ideals. Do you think large international sporting events like the Olympics do more good than harm in today's world? What changes, if any, would make them more aligned with their founding ideals?
